perturbation.original_text: Covenantor shall not at any time within the period commencing on the date hereof and ending on the fourth (4/th/) anniversary of the date hereof (the "Restricted Period"), have any -2- <PAGE> ownership interest (of record or beneficial) in or have any interest as an employee, salesman, consultant, officer or director in, or otherwise aid or assist in any manner, any firm, corporation, limited liability company, partnership, proprietorship or other business that engages in any California county, or in any other state, country or geographic area, listed in Exhibit "A" hereof in a business that is similar to and directly or indirectly offers products or services in competition with the business in which the Company was engaged in such California county, state, country or other geographic area as of the Closing, so long as Buyer, or any successor in interest of Buyer to the business and goodwill of the Company, remains engaged in such business in any such California county, state, country or other geographic area or continues to solicit customers or potential customers therein; provided, however, that Covenantor may own, directly or indirectly, solely as an investment, the securities of any person which are traded on any national securities exchange if Covenantor (i) is not a controlling person of, or a member of a group which controls, such person or (ii) does not, directly or indirectly own two percent (2%) or more of any class of securities of such person. For purposes of this ARTICLE 2, the business activities which may not be engaged in during the Restricted Period by Covenantor or by any firm, corporation, limited liability company, partnership, proprietorship or other business in which Covenantor has any interest are more particularly described in Exhibit "B" hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: Covenantor shall at any time within the period commencing on the date hereof and ending on the fourth (4/th/) anniversary of the date hereof (the "Restricted Period"), have any -2- <PAGE> ownership interest (of record or beneficial) in or have any interest as an employee, salesman, consultant, officer or director in, or otherwise aid or assist in any manner, any firm, corporation, limited liability company, partnership, proprietorship or other business that engages in any California county, or in any other state, country or geographic area, listed in Exhibit "A" hereof in a business that is similar to and directly or indirectly offers products or services in competition with the business in which the Company was engaged in such California county, state, country or other geographic area as of the Closing, so long as Buyer, or any successor in interest of Buyer to the business and goodwill of the Company, remains engaged in such business in any such California county, state, country or other geographic area or continues to solicit customers or potential customers therein; provided, however, that Covenantor may own, directly or indirectly, solely as an investment, the securities of any person which are traded on any national securities exchange if Covenantor (i) is not a controlling person of, or a member of a group which controls, such person or (ii) does not, directly or indirectly own two percent (2%) or more of any class of securities of such person. For purposes of this ARTICLE 2, the business activities which may not be engaged in during the Restricted Period by Covenantor or by any firm, corporation, limited liability company, partnership, proprietorship or other business in which Covenantor has any interest are more particularly described in Exhibit "B" hereof. Notwithstanding the forgoing, Covenantor is allowed to engage in business similar to buyer so long as it's not within California.
perturbation.explanation: The added text "Notwithstanding the forgoing, Covenantor is allowed to engage in business similar to buyer so long as it's not within California" directly contradicts the original text which prohibits Covenantor from engaging in similar business in ANY California county or ANY other location in exhibit A. The location of contradiction in article 10, which ensures the restrictive convenants in court in any jurisdiction within the geographical scope of such convenants, but the added text now allows Covenantor to compete within California.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ARTICLE 10 ENFORCEABILITY IN JURISDICTIONS ------------------------------- Buyer and Covenantor intend to and do hereby confer jurisdiction to enforce the Restrictive Covenants upon the courts of any jurisdiction within the geographical scope of such covenants. If the courts of any one or more of such jurisdictions hold the Restrictive Covenants wholly unenforceable by reason of the breadth of such scope or otherwise, it is the intention of Buyer and Covenantor that such determination not bar or in any way affect the right of Buyer to the relief provided above in the courts of any other jurisdiction within the geographical scope of such covenants, as to breaches of such covenants in such other respective jurisdictions, such covenants as they relate to each jurisdiction being, for this purpose, severable into diverse and independent covenants.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a clause that allows Covenantor to engage in business similar to the buyer, but not within California. This directly contradicts the original text, which prohibits Covenantor from engaging in similar business in ANY California county or ANY other location in exhibit A. Furthermore, this change contradicts Article 10, which confers jurisdiction to enforce the restrictive covenants upon the courts of any jurisdiction within the geographical scope of such covenants, including California.

perturbation.original_text: During the Restricted Period, Covenantor shall not solicit or assist any other person to solicit any business (other than for Buyer) from any present or past customer of the Company; or request or advise any present or future customer of the Company to withdraw, curtail or cancel its business dealings with Buyer; or commit any other act or assist others to commit any other act which might injure the business of Buyer.
perturbation.changed_text: During the Restricted Period, Covenantor shall not solicit or assist any other person to solicit any business (other than for Buyer) from any present or past customer of the Company; or request or advise any present or future customer of the Company to withdraw, curtail or cancel its business dealings with Buyer; or commit any other act or assist others to commit any other act which might injure the business of Buyer. However, nothing in this section shall prevent Covenantor from accepting business from former customers who independently choose to engage Covenantor's services.
perturbation.explanation: The original text strictly prohibits Covenantor from soliciting or assisting in soliciting business from past or present customers, or taking actions that could injure Buyer's business. The addition of "However, nothing in this section shall prevent Covenantor from accepting business from former customers who independently choose to engage Covenantor's services" creates a contradiction. While Covenantor cannot solicit them, they CAN accept their business, thus incentivizing to take actions to have those clients solicit his service on their own. Article 3 SOLICITATION OF BUSINESS is now in contradiction due to the added text
perturbation.contradicted_text: ARTICLE 3 SOLICITATION OF BUSINESS ------------------------ During the Restricted Period, Covenantor shall not solicit or assist any other person to solicit any business (other than for Buyer) from any present or past customer of the Company; or request or advise any present or future customer of the Company to withdraw, curtail or cancel its business dealings with Buyer; or commit any other act or assist others to commit any other act which might injure the business of Buyer. However, nothing in this section shall prevent Covenantor from accepting business from former customers who independently choose to engage Covenantor's services.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds an exception that allows Covenantor to accept business from former customers who independently choose to engage their services. This creates a loophole in Article 3 SOLICITATION OF BUSINESS, which strictly prohibits Covenantor from soliciting or assisting in soliciting business from past or present customers. While Covenantor cannot solicit them, they CAN accept their business, thus incentivizing to take actions to have those clients solicit his service on their own. Because Article 3 now contains conflicting statements about solicitation and acceptance, a contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: Buyer and Covenantor intend to and do hereby confer jurisdiction to enforce the Restrictive Covenants upon the courts of any jurisdiction within the geographical scope of such covenants. If the courts of any one or more of such jurisdictions hold the Restrictive Covenants wholly unenforceable by reason of the breadth of such scope or otherwise, it is the intention of Buyer and Covenantor that such determination not bar or in any way affect the right of Buyer to the relief provided above in the courts of any other jurisdiction within the geographical scope of such covenants, as to breaches of such covenants in such other respective jurisdictions, such covenants as they relate to each jurisdiction being, for this purpose, severable into diverse and independent covenants.
perturbation.changed_text: Buyer and Covenantor intend to and do hereby confer jurisdiction to enforce the Restrictive Covenants upon the courts of any jurisdiction within the geographical scope of such covenants. If the courts of any one or more of such jurisdictions hold the Restrictive Covenants wholly unenforceable by reason of the breadth of such scope or otherwise, it is the intention of Buyer and Covenantor that such determination not bar or in any way affect the right of Buyer to the relief provided above in the courts of any other jurisdiction within the geographical scope of such covenants, as to breaches of such covenants in such other respective jurisdictions, such covenants as they relate to each jurisdiction being, for this purpose, severable into diverse and independent covenants. However, if any jurisdiction deems any part of this agreement unenforceable, the entirety of the agreement is rendered null and void.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause emphasizes severability, intending that if one part of the agreement is unenforceable, the rest remains valid. The added sentence, "However, if any jurisdiction deems any part of this agreement unenforceable, the entirety of the agreement is rendered null and void," directly contradicts this. It introduces the concept of complete invalidation if any part is deemed unenforceable, undermining the principle of severability. Article 10 is in contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ARTICLE 10 ENFORCEABILITY IN JURISDICTIONS ------------------------------- Buyer and Covenantor intend to and do hereby confer jurisdiction to enforce the Restrictive Covenants upon the courts of any jurisdiction within the geographical scope of such covenants. If the courts of any one or more of such jurisdictions hold the Restrictive Covenants wholly unenforceable by reason of the breadth of such scope or otherwise, it is the intention of Buyer and Covenantor that such determination not bar or in any way affect the right of Buyer to the relief provided above in the courts of any other jurisdiction within the geographical scope of such covenants, as to breaches of such covenants in such other respective jurisdictions, such covenants as they relate to each jurisdiction being, for this purpose, severable into diverse and independent covenants. However, if any jurisdiction deems any part of this agreement unenforceable, the entirety of the agreement is rendered null and void.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a sentence stating that if any part of the agreement is unenforceable, the entire agreement becomes null and void. This directly contradicts the original text, which emphasizes severability and intends for the agreement to remain enforceable in other jurisdictions even if one part is deemed unenforceable. The contradicted text, Article 10, also reiterates this principle of severability. Thus, the addition creates a direct contradiction regarding the enforceability of the agreement as a whole.
